requests.name: List Applications
requests.description: Enumerates apps added to your organization with pagination. A subset of apps can be returned that match a supported filter expression or query.

queryParams.description: Specifies the pagination cursor for the next page of apps
Specifies the number of results for a page
Filters apps by status, user.id, group.id or credentials.signing.kid expression
Traverses users link relationship and optionally embeds Application User resource

requests.name: Add Application
requests.description: Adds a new application to your Okta organization.

queryParams.description: Executes activation lifecycle operation when creating the app

requests.name: Delete Application
requests.description: Removes an inactive application.

requests.name: Get Application
requests.description: Fetches an application from your Okta organization by `id`.

queryParams.description: Traverses users link relationship and optionally embeds Application User resource

requests.name: Update Application
requests.description: Updates an application in your organization.

requests.name: List Key Credentials for Application
requests.description: Enumerates key credentials for an application

requests.name: Get Key Credential for Application
requests.description: Gets a specific [application key credential](#application-key-credential-model) by `kid`

requests.name: Clone Application Key Credential
requests.description: Clones a X.509 certificate for an application key credential from a source application to target application.

queryParams.description: Unique key of the target Application

requests.name: List Users Assigned to Application

requests.name: Assign User to Application for SSO & Provisioning
requests.description: Assigns an user to an application with [credentials](#application-user-credentials-object) and an app-specific [profile](#application-user-profile-object). Profile mappings defined for the application are first applied before applying any profile properties specified in the request.

requests.name: Remove User from Application

queryParams.description: Traverses users link relationship and optionally embeds Application User resource

requests.name: Fetch a list of events from your Okta organization system log.
requests.description: The Okta System Log API provides read access to your organization’s system log. This API provides more functionality than the Events API

queryParams.description: Filters apps by status, user.id, group.id or credentials.signing.kid expression
Specifies the number of results for a page
Specifies the pagination cursor for the next page of apps

requests.name: Create Session with Session Token
requests.description: Creates a new session for a user with a valid session token. Use this API if, for example, you want to set the session cookie yourself instead of allowing Okta to set it, or want to hold the session ID in order to delete a session via the API instead of visiting the logout URL.
Get details about a session.

requests.name: List Users
requests.description: Lists users in your organization with pagination in most cases.  A subset of users can be returned that match a supported filter expression or search criteria.

queryParams.description: Specifies the pagination cursor for the next page of apps
Specifies the number of results for a page
Filters apps by status, user.id, group.id or credentials.signing.kid expression
Searches for users with a supported filtering  expression for most properties
Traverses users link relationship and optionally embeds Application User resource

requests.name: Create User
requests.description: Creates a new user in your Okta organization with or without credentials.

queryParams.description: Executes activation lifecycle operation when creating the app
Indicates whether to create a user with a specified authentication provider
With activate=true, set nextLogin to "changePassword" to have the password be EXPIRED, so user must change it the next time they log in.

requests.name: Delete User
requests.description: Deletes a user permanently.  This operation can only be performed on users that have a `DEPROVISIONED` status.  **This action cannot be recovered!**

requests.name: Get User
requests.description: Fetches a user from your Okta organization.

requests.name: Update User
requests.description: Update a user's profile and/or credentials using strict-update semantics.

requests.name: Get Assigned App Links
requests.description: Fetches appLinks for all direct or indirect (via group membership) assigned applications.

requests.name: Change Password
requests.description: Changes a user's password by validating the user's current password.  This operation can only be performed on users in `STAGED`, `ACTIVE`, `PASSWORD_EXPIRED`, or `RECOVERY` status that have a valid [password credential](#password-object)

requests.name: Change Recovery Question
requests.description: Changes a user's recovery question & answer credential by validating the user's current password.  This operation can only be performed on users in **STAGED**, **ACTIVE** or **RECOVERY** `status` that have a valid [password credential](#password-object)

requests.name: Forgot Password
requests.description: Generates a one-time token (OTT) that can be used to reset a user's password.  The user will be required to validate their security question's answer when visiting the reset link.  This operation can only be performed on users with a valid [recovery question credential](#recovery-question-object) and have an `ACTIVE` status.
Enumerates all the enrolled factors for the specified user

requests.name: Enroll Factor
requests.description: Enrolls a user with a supported [factor](#list-factors-to-enroll)

queryParams.description: id of SMS template (only for SMS factor)
Executes activation lifecycle operation when creating the app

requests.description: Enumerates all the [supported factors](#supported-factors-for-providers) that can be enrolled for the specified user
Enumerates all available security questions for a user's `question` factor
Unenrolls an existing factor for the specified user, allowing the user to enroll a new factor.
Fetches a factor for the specified user

requests.name: Activate Factor
requests.description: The `sms` and `token:software:totp` [factor types](#factor-type) require activation to complete the enrollment process.

requests.name: Verify MFA Factor
requests.description: Verifies an OTP for a `token` or `token:hardware` factor

queryParams.description: id of SMS template (only for SMS factor)

requests.name: Get Member Groups
requests.description: Fetches the groups of which the user is a member.

queryParams.description: Specifies the pagination cursor for the next page of apps
Specifies the number of results for a page

requests.name: Activate User
requests.description: Activates a user.  This operation can only be performed on users with a `STAGED` status.  Activation of a user is an asynchronous operation.  The user will have the `transitioningToStatus` property with a value of `ACTIVE` during activation to indicate that the user hasn't completed the asynchronous operation.  The user will have a status of `ACTIVE` when the activation process is complete.

requests.name: Deactivate User
requests.description: Deactivates a user.  This operation can only be performed on users that do not have a `DEPROVISIONED` status.  Deactivation of a user is an asynchronous operation.  The user will have the `transitioningToStatus` property with a value of `DEPROVISIONED` during deactivation to indicate that the user hasn't completed the asynchronous operation.  The user will have a status of `DEPROVISIONED` when the deactivation process is complete.

requests.name: Expire Password
requests.description: This operation transitions the user to the status of `PASSWORD_EXPIRED` so that the user is required to change their password at their next login.

queryParams.description: Sets the user's password to a temporary password,  if true

requests.name: Reset Factors
requests.description: This operation resets all factors for the specified user. All MFA factor enrollments returned to the unenrolled state. The user's status remains ACTIVE. This link is present only if the user is currently enrolled in one or more MFA factors.

requests.name: Reset Password
requests.description: Generates a one-time token (OTT) that can be used to reset a user's password.  The OTT link can be automatically emailed to the user or returned to the API caller and distributed using a custom flow.

queryParams.description: Indicates whether to create a user with a specified authentication provider

requests.name: Suspend User
requests.description: Suspends a user.  This operation can only be performed on users with an `ACTIVE` status.  The user will have a status of `SUSPENDED` when the process is complete.

requests.name: Unlock User
requests.description: Unlocks a user with a `LOCKED_OUT` status and returns them to `ACTIVE` status.  Users will be able to login with their current password.

requests.name: Unsuspend User
requests.description: Unsuspends a user and returns them to the `ACTIVE` state.  This operation can only be performed on users that have a `SUSPENDED` status.
Lists all roles assigned to a user.

queryParams.description: Traverses users link relationship and optionally embeds Application User resource

requests.description: Assigns a role to a user.
Unassigns a role from a user.

queryParams.description: Specifies the pagination cursor for the next page of apps
Specifies the number of results for a page

requests.description: Removes all active identity provider sessions. This forces the user to authenticate on the next operation. Optionally revokes OpenID Connect and OAuth refresh and access tokens issued to the user.

queryParams.description: Revoke issued OpenID Connect and OAuth refresh and access tokens
